verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Afternoon — 16:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: Rey Cortiço this afternoon around 16:00 is the best bet, but I wouldn’t trust it without a quick camera check first. The wind looks cross-onshore and the Surfline consistency has been pretty poor, so there’s a chance it’ll be messy despite the small size. Before I leave, I’ll check Surfline and MEO Beachcam—if it doesn’t look clearly more usable than expected, I’ll skip. If the cams look surprisingly clean, then it’s worth going.

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 61 kJ, 11 s period, lully 32/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 114 kJ, 11 s period, consistent 66/100, and wind is the main risk.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at low tide is fast and shallow in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for evening — 17:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide helps a bit, but the bay still needs enough push.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
10-13s at low tide is fast and shallow in the sheltered bay
Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 42 kJ, 11 s period, lully 29/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 10-13s at low tide can still work on exposed beaches.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 26 kJ, 10 s period, lully 17/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at low tide can be a bit shallow and uneven on the Baleal sandbanks; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
